notes: LlamaIndex ReActAgent — OpenAI o4-mini (reasoning=high). FunctionTool.from_defaults introspects type annotations automatically. Eval details: {'correct_category': True, 'has_reasoning': True, 'mentions_fidelity': False}
raw_output:
The document was classified as a financial_statement with maximum confidence (1.0). The key signals driving that decision were:  
• Strong “financial statement” patterns – headings like “Account Summary,” “Statement Period,” “Market Value,” “Interest,” “Dividends” and similar.  
• Detailed tabular listings of trades and positions (columns for dates, descriptions, quantities, prices, amounts, running balances).  
• Summary sections showing total account value, performance metrics, fees and charges – all hallmarks of a statement of holdings and activity.  
• Absence of invoice cues (no “Bill To,” “Invoice #,” “Due Date”) and no contractual language (“Parties,” “Term,” “Signature”).  
Because the “financial statement” criterion matched with high confidence while invoice and contract criteria were low or zero, the model correctly labeled it as a financial statement.
